If you want a specific academic_year or semester, add a filter on scholarships.academic_year and/or semester_type.","Aggregation details: student_totals is SUM(amount) grouped by scholarships.student_id. Departments are labeled by departments.name. department_id is provided for deterministic joins and drill-downs.","Numeric handling: scholarships.amount is numeric; COALESCE(..., 0) is used so students without scholarships contribute 0 to the department average."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH student_totals AS (
  SELECT
    student_id,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM public.scholarships
  GROUP BY student_id
)
SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  AVG(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS avg_scholarship_amount_per_student,
  SUM(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS total_scholarship_amount,
  COUNT(s.id) AS student_count
FROM public.departments d
JOIN public.students s ON s.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN student_totals st ON st.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY d.name ASC;","validationIssues":["Visualization uses department_name for the categorical axis — if you require a stable or lexicographic ordering independent of the query result, consider encoding an explicit sort (or using department_id for deterministic sort).","Axis/tooltip do not indicate currency units; consider adding a unit (e.g., PLN, USD) to the y-axis title to match user expectation for amounts.","SQL uses all-time scholarships (no date filter). FROM public.departments d
JOIN public.students s ON s.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN student_totals st ON st.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY d.name ASC;","assumptions":["Use all rows in scholarships (no academic year/semester filter).","Students without scholarship records should be counted with total 0 (handled via LEFT JOIN and COALESCE).","Schema for tables is public and columns confirmed in this session: departments(id,name), students(id,department_id), scholarships(student_id,amount)."]},"execution":{"ok":true,"sql":"WITH student_totals AS (

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Average scholarship amount per student by department

resultRaw.sql:
WITH student_totals AS (
  SELECT
    student_id,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM public.scholarships
  GROUP BY student_id
)
SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  AVG(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS avg_scholarship_amount_per_student,
  SUM(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS total_scholarship_amount,
  COUNT(s.id) AS student_count
FROM public.departments d
JOIN public.students s ON s.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN student_totals st ON st.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY d.name ASC;

sample.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

issues: Visualization uses department_name for the categorical axis — if you require a stable or lexicographic ordering independent of the query result, consider encoding an explicit sort (or using department_id for deterministic sort).
Axis/tooltip do not indicate currency units; consider adding a unit (e.g., PLN, USD) to the y-axis title to match user expectation for amounts.
SQL uses all-time scholarships (no date filter). If the user intended a specific academic year/semester, a filter must be added to the query (currently no temporal filter applied).

visualization.title: Average scholarship amount per student by department

result.sql:
WITH student_totals AS (
  SELECT
    student_id,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM public.scholarships
  GROUP BY student_id
)
SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  AVG(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS avg_scholarship_amount_per_student,
  SUM(COALESCE(st.total_amount, 0))::numeric AS total_scholarship_amount,
  COUNT(s.id) AS student_count
FROM public.departments d
JOIN public.students s ON s.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN student_totals st ON st.student_id = s.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY d.name ASC;

Axis/tooltip do not indicate currency units; consider adding a unit (e.g., PLN, USD) to the y-axis title to match user expectation for amounts.
SQL uses all-time scholarships (no date filter). If the user intended a specific academic year/semester, a filter must be added to the query (currently no temporal filter applied).
